steps.title: Find the median position for data set A
steps.content:
Data set A contains 41 values, so the median is at position $(41+1)\div2=21$. Track the running positions:
  • Value 0: position 1; cumulative total 1
  • Value 1: positions 2-8; cumulative total 8
  • Value 2: positions 9-16; cumulative total 16
  • Value 3: positions 17-25; cumulative total 25
Position 21 is in positions 17-25, so median A = 3.

steps.title: Compare with the median of data set B
steps.content: Data set B removes the single 13, leaving 40 values. The median is the average of positions 20 and 21. Both positions still fall in the value-3 group, so median B is $(3+3)\div2=3$. Since both medians equal 3, Choice C — The median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A. is correct.
